digest.component_or_area: example claude-skills TDD test-design standard and code-review tests criterion
propositions: The three example skills carry inline TDD prose that is strong on RED-to-GREEN integrity but under-specified on selecting the right test (no coverage inventory, tier rule, invariant oracle, or fault-realism guidance).
A single shared test-design standard should be created at examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md and loaded by all three skills where each incorporates testing.

The code-review tests criterion should add two uncovered antipatterns (non-gating test; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync), each with explicit false-positive guards and no posture or routing change.
The eval suite should gain recall and no-fire cases for both new antipatterns so it owns the new rubric surface.
Held-out test discipline must be preserved so the fix/implementation subagent never sees held-out test code, verified mechanically via grep and diff.
Plan-review E5, criteria_routing.json, and any threshold/posture fields must remain untouched, and CI gates for lint, prompt-index drift, and criteria-routing parity must stay green.
